enriched_metadata.display_name: Heart Disease Dataset - UCI Medical Classification
enriched_metadata.objective: Predict the presence of heart disease in patients based on 13 clinical attributes including demographics, symptoms, and diagnostic test results
enriched_metadata.sources: UCI Heart Disease database combining data from Cleveland Clinic, Hungarian Institute of Cardiology, University Hospital Zurich, and Long Beach VA Medical Center
enriched_metadata.features_description: 13 clinical attributes: age, sex, chest pain type, resting blood pressure, serum cholesterol, fasting blood sugar, resting ECG, maximum heart rate, exercise-induced angina, ST depression, ST slope, number of major vessels, and thalassemia
enriched_metadata.representativity_description: Clinical data from multiple international hospitals (Cleveland, Hungary, Switzerland, Long Beach) with 303 patients and binary heart disease outcome
enriched_metadata.sample_balance_description: Binary classification dataset with presence/absence of heart disease; distribution reflects clinical population with both healthy and diseased patients
enriched_metadata.missing_values_description: No missing values in processed dataset - cleaned version of original UCI data with 76 attributes reduced to 14 most relevant features
enriched_metadata.data_errors_description: High-quality medical dataset with complete clinical measurements from multiple hospitals; patient identification information anonymized
